texto: Happy hour?" Jason says. "It's barely noon, Grams""Oh, shush, you. You'll have some, yes?'"Well"-he smiles slyly and wiggles his eyebrows-"if you insist". Every time, it's the same thing. Leaning in, he rubs his hands together expectantly. The drinking age in New York State was raised last year, so technically, I suppose, this is still illegal for my grandson. But the Jews didn't spend forty years wandering the desert so that I could forfeit a gin and tonic with my progeny...
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Susan Jane Gilman que provoca passagem do tempo e prioridades. Ao questionar “Happy hour?" Jason says. "It's barely noon, Grams""Oh, shush, you. You'll have some, yes?'"Well"-…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre felicidade, gin-and-tonic, wit, com foco em termino e wit.

Every time, it's the same thing. Leaning in, he rubs his hands together expectantly. The drinking age in New York State was raised last year, so technically, I suppose, this is still illegal for my grandson. But the Jews didn't spend forty years wandering the desert so that I could forfeit a gin and tonic with my progeny...

texto: Every woman should see herself looking uniquely breathtaking, in something tailored to celebrate her body, so that she is better able to appreciate her own beauty and better equipped to withstand the ideals of our narrow-waisted, narrow- minded culture.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Susan Jane Gilman sobre temas ligados a motivacao. A afirmação “Every woman should see herself looking uniquely breathtaking, in something tailored to celebrate …” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a motivacao, inspiration, vida, útil em momentos de inspiracao e vida.
